title: Mushroom vol-au-vents
subtitle: Puff pastry cases filled with a creamy mushroom sauce
excerpt: Vol-au-vents: puff pastry cases cut with two cutters (one to cut the base, one to score the lid), baked until puffed and hollow, filled with a creamy chestnut mushroom sauce. Simple in concept, impressive in result. The pastry cases can be baked ahead.
sourceNotes: Vol-au-vents (French: 'windblown', referring to the lightness of the pastry) are documented in French cookery from the early nineteenth century, attributed to the chef Antonin Carême. They appear in English-language cookery references from the mid-nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, includes vol-au-vent recipes. The mushroom filling in a cream sauce is a widely reproduced domestic form from the twentieth century and is fully public-domain.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the cases up to 2 days ahead and keep in an airtight container. Fill just before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 18 to 20 minutes until puffed and deep golden

glossaryTerms.definition: A vol-au-vent case is made from two layers of pastry: a base disc that is solid, and a ring that sits on top to form the walls. Cut a large circle from one layer of pastry for the base. Cut an identical circle for the second layer, then cut out the centre with a smaller cutter to leave a ring. Brush the base disc with egg wash and press the ring on top. The two-cutter method gives a deep, well-defined wall. Do not egg-wash the cut edges or the layers will stick and the pastry will not puff upward.

content.text: Vol-au-vents are quicker than they look. The pastry cases take 20 minutes of prep and 20 minutes in the oven. The filling is a 10-minute job. Make the cases a day ahead and fill just before serving.
Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. See the

method. Cut 12 large circles (about 7 cm) from half the {{puff-pastry}} for the bases. From the other half, cut 12 matching circles and cut out the centres with a smaller cutter (about 5 cm). Brush the bases lightly with beaten {{eggs}}, avoiding the edges. Press the rings firmly onto each base. Chill for 15 minutes. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes until puffed and deep golden. Press down the lids to reveal the hollow centre if necessary.

Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in a saucepan over high heat. Fry the sliced {{mushrooms-chestnut}} until all the liquid has evaporated and the mushrooms are golden. Add the thyme leaves from the {{thyme}} sprigs and stir for 30 seconds. Stir in the {{plain-flour}} and cook for 1 minute. Gradually add the {{double-cream}} and {{whole-milk}}, stirring until smooth and thickened. Season well. Spoon into the warm cases and serve immediately.
